channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Elon Musk confirma las antiguas preocupaciones sobre los superricos
item.description: Platón hizo una propuesta radical sobre la riqueza que resuena mucho en nuestros tiempos.
item.dc:creator: David Lay Williams

item.title: Neighborhood Reels From Loss of Landmark Church That Stood for 173 Years
item.description: The South Bushwick Church, whose steeple was a local beacon, was heavily damaged in a fire on Friday in a blow to a Brooklyn neighborhood known for its architectural gems.
item.dc:creator: Cassidy Jensen

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The South Bushwick Church on Bushwick Avenue, completed in 1853, was a New York City landmark.

item.title: $50 Million Spent on N.Y. Primaries Includes A.I. and AIPAC Money
item.description: As early voting began earlier this month, AIPAC emerged from the sidelines to funnel $650,000 to Representative Adriano Espaillat, a candidate opposed by a democratic socialist.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Adriano Espaillat’s long support of Israel and his acceptance of AIPAC money have become attack lines for his primary opponent, Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 23, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: At World Cup Soccer Game, Palestinian Emblems Abound
item.description: Jordan’s first qualification for the World Cup also means a World Cup berth for Palestinians, millions of whom live in the country.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Carlos Avila Gonzalez/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Fans of Jordan during the World Cup game against Algeria in Santa Clara, Calif., on Monday.

item.title: Late Night Dunks on Trump for Hiring Greenwater Services
item.description: Jimmy Fallon poked fun at the name of the business under fire for turning the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool bright green: “Nailed it.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)
Last Week Tonight With John Oliver (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Por qué cada vez más adultos mayores eligen el divorcio en EE. UU.
item.description: Cada vez más personas de la tercera edad deciden poner fin a sus matrimonios, y los expertos tienen varias teorías sobre por qué.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Pearson

item.title: 7 composiciones de Mozart desconocidas hasta ahora aparecen en un cuaderno
item.description: Un cuaderno que acaba de salir a la luz recoge las clases que el compositor impartía a una alumna e incluye siete composiciones hasta ahora desconocidas.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Arlo Brown

media:content.media:description: Páginas del cuaderno manuscrito de Mozart en la Biblioteca Nacional de Francia.

item.title: Word of the Day: hauteur
item.description: This word has appeared in 14 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Qué es un domo de calor y por qué está aumentando las temperaturas en Europa
item.description: Este fenómeno meteorológico, común en distintas regiones del mundo, está provocando varios días de calor cercano a niveles récord en el Reino Unido, España y Francia.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:description: Un bañista en París intenta refrescarse el domingo, en medio de la segunda gran ola de calor del año en Europa.

item.title: In Colombia, a Right-Wing Wildcard Rises
item.description: A right-wing victory in Latin America’s third-largest country could ripple across the region.
item.dc:creator: Michael Shifter

item.title: Trump’s Iran Debacle Could Be a Gift for America
item.description: There is at least a chance that America will not return to war with Iran, but find a way to turn the page on 47 years of animosity and confrontation.
item.dc:creator: Robert Malley and Stephen Wertheim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: La misión militar de EE. UU. en la frontera con México no tiene previsto un final
item.description: El patrullaje ha ayudado a empujar a los cárteles y a los traficantes a zonas más remotas. Pero a los analistas les preocupa que las misiones fronterizas puedan restar atención al entrenamiento, agotar los recursos y mermar la preparación.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un soldado destinado a la frontera sur, cerca de Santa Teresa, Nuevo México, el año pasado

item.title: Those British Strawberries Are Being Picked by Central Asian Workers
item.description: Ten years after Brexit, most seasonal workers in Britain are from countries such as Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan. Without them, agricultural chiefs say, many farms would fail.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle and Aigerim Turgunbaeva

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shukrat Djuraev at Homefield Farm, in Kent, England, this month.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Starmer Exits With His Rivals at the Gates
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, June 23, 2026

item.title: Brexit Has Cost the UK Growth, Analysts Say, in the Decade Since the Vote
item.description: Citing lower trade and investment, analysts broadly agree that Britain’s economy is smaller than it would have been if the country had stayed in the E.U.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ten years ago, British voters approved by a slim margin a proposal for the country to leave the European Union. The economic results, economists say, have not been glowing.

item.title: Two Dead After Shooting at California Library
item.description: A gunman used a firearm inside the library while it was open, killing two and injuring another person before being taken into custody, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:description: A 911 call shortly after 5 p.m. reported a shooting at the library.

item.title: Danny McBride on Why It’s So Hard for Art to Break Through
item.description: “Nothing has its moment in the sun. And I think that that’s probably just because of the world we live in. There’s just so much stuff out there, old and new,” Danny McBride says on “The Interview.”

item.title: As Vance Leads Iran Negotiations, Trump Creates Disruptions in His Path
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is in a politically precarious spot.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: How Vice President JD Vance handles the future of the negotiations will factor into Republicans’ performance in the midterm elections and his future as a potential successor to President Trump.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 23, 2026
item.description: Kathy Bloomer won’t give us a straight answer.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Think you can see right through the deceptive theme in today’s puzzle?

item.title: Judge Blocks Bans on Using Food Stamps for Sugary Drinks and Candy
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Agriculture Department lacked the authority to approve state waivers that restrict what SNAP participants can buy with their benefits.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu
item.category: National Center for Law and Economic Justice

media:content.media:credit: Nic Antaya for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The decision on Monday, by a federal judge in Washington, was a rollback of restrictions that officials have characterized as a major achievement of the Make America Healthy Again movement.

item.title: Storms Disrupt World Cup Matches in Philadelphia and New Jersey
item.description: The ponchos came out as fans did what they could to stay dry. The France-Iraq match in Philadelphia endured a lengthy weather delay.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:description: The soggy France-Iraq match in Philadelphia made it to halftime, but lightning strikes in the area prompted a delay.

item.title: Collapsed Surfside Condo Had Been Failing for Weeks, Investigators Find
item.description: Two columns that held up the building’s pool deck began to fail several weeks before the 2021 collapse, according to a new federal report.
item.dc:creator: Mike Baker
item.category: National Institute of Standards and Technology

media:content.media:description: The Champlain Towers South condo building in Surfside, Fla., in 2021.

item.title: Dark Smoke in a Sunny Place: Neighbors of L.A. Fire Struggle for Breath
item.description: The plume from the stubborn blaze in a cold-storage facility has dissipated, but people in the Boyle Heights neighborhood say they are in a toxic miasma.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín and Maia Spoto

media:content.media:description: The towering plume of smoke dominated the Los Angeles skyline.

item.title: Trump on the Shabby Condition of the Reflecting Pool: Not My Fault
item.description: President Trump said the blooms of green algae and the peeling polyurethane had nothing to do with the rushed $16.4 million makeover he had ordered.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Maxine Joselow

media:content.media:description: Ongoing work on Sunday at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which has been troubled for decades by leaks and algae blooms that turn its water green.

item.title: Police Officer Killed in Shootout in Montreal
item.description: On Monday, a police officer was killed during a shootout. A civilian was killed and another police officer was wounded, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: In New York City, Early Voting Saw Major Changes From 2025
item.description: Turnout was low and the electorate was much older than it was last year when the mayoral race between Mamdani and Cuomo fueled heavy early voting.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang and Nicholas Fandos

item.title: Abortion Rights Group Endorses Platner Over Collins in Maine
item.description: Senator Susan Collins, a Republican, drew criticism from Planned Parenthood for voting to confirm Justice Brett Kavanaugh, who helped overturn Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times; Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Democrat who is seeking to unseat Senator Susan Collins, and Alexis McGill Johnson, the president and chief executive of Planned Parenthood.

item.title: La razón secreta por la que los jefes quieren que todos vuelvan a la oficina
item.description: La reticencia a cualquier tipo de trabajo remoto quizá se deba a algo muy específico: el ego
item.dc:creator: Adam Grant, Marissa Shandell and Courtney Elliott

item.title: High School Students Heckle Bard President Over His Ties With Epstein
item.description: At a graduation ceremony for high schools affiliated with the college, Leon Botstein said, “To get anything done, you’re going to have to dance with the devil.”
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag and Caitlyn Freeman

media:content.media:credit: Cole Wilson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bard College operates four high schools in New York City and another in Newark.

item.title: Tesla Crash That Killed a Texas Woman Will be Investigated by Federal Regulators
item.description: The car’s driver-assistance system was in use when the crash killed a woman on Friday, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McCarthy

media:content.media:description: Tesla’s driver-assistance systems have been implicated in a number of accidents, some of them fatal.

item.title: Keir Starmer renuncia como primer ministro del Reino Unido. ¿Qué sigue ahora?
item.description: Su salida allana el camino para que Andy Burnham, un popular exalcalde del Partido Laborista, se convierta en el séptimo primer ministro del país en una década.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer outside 10 Downing Street in London where he announced his resignation on Monday.

item.title: Iran Team Leaves Locker Room Note: The ‘Spirit of Iran Remains Alive and Steadfast’
item.description: After fighting Belgium to a draw for one of Iran’s best World Cup results, the Iranians had a note for their fans and the world.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Iran national team has been allowed into the United States only for about 48 hours to play its games and then must leave the country.

item.title: Clive Davis Knew to Let Patti Smith Have Free Rein
item.description: For Patti Smith, the best guidance was something that Davis rarely granted: free rein.
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles

media:content.media:credit: Ron Galella, Ltd./Ron Galella Collection, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Patti Smith and Clive Davis in 1993.

item.title: Protester Is Struck by Driver Outside Immigration Detention Center
item.description: The Delaney Hall facility in New Jersey has been the site of daily protests for nearly a month. Police officials in Newark are continuing to investigate the incident.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley

item.title: U.S. Eases Sanctions on Iran’s Oil
item.description: Also, Alan Greenspan dies at 100. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Switzerland today.

item.title: With Win in Washington, Socialists Have Momentum in Urban America
item.description: Democratic socialists, harnessing generational frustration over affordability, lead New York and Seattle and are knocking on the door in Los Angeles and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson, Jill Cowan and Anna Griffin

media:content.media:description: “I don’t buy that the status quo is all we can do,” Janeese Lewis George, a City Council member in Washington, D.C., said voters told her ahead of her victory in the mayoral primary.

item.title: Federal Citizenship Data Tool Cannot Be Used to Screen Voters, Judge Rules
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration’s providing federal data to states to check and purge their voter rolls violated several laws prohibiting the disclosure.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voting booths in Cypress, Texas, last month.

item.title: Looking Back on the U.S.A.’s Many Founders, 250 Years In
item.description: In the past 50 years, the way we tell the story of the Revolution has become dramatically more complex. Can it still inspire us all?
item.dc:creator: Jane Kamensky
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Universal History Archive/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The five-man drafting committee presenting the Declaration of Independence to Congress, in an early 19th-century painting by John Trumbull.

item.title: School Year Cut Short and Aid Delivery Slowed Amid Fuel Crisis in Cuba
item.description: A U.S. oil blockade imposed by the Trump administration has set off an increasingly agonizing energy crisis that has brought transportation largely to a standstill. In an effort to save energy resources, the government ended the school year early.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:credit: Lisette Poole González for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Analeidis Arias Matos getting her son, Alejandro, ready for school last month in Santiago, Cuba.

item.title: The Itinerant Preacher Who Helped Secure the Separation of Church and State
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Marjoleine Kars

item.title: The Enslaved Woman Who Sued for Freedom and Emancipated Herself
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Martha S. Jones
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Britons React to Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Resignation Announcement
item.description: After Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that he is resigning from his position, Britons shared their thoughts about a change of leadership.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: The Man of Faith Who Heard a Righteous Call in the Founding Credo
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Annette Gordon-Reed

item.title: Why Democrats Need a Politics of Joy
item.description: The joylessness of metrics-obsessed, achievement-oriented elite culture is eroding the simple joys of living, the novelist Gary Shteyngart argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.” And, he says, this antipathy toward pleasure is a problem for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: The Patriot Housewife Whose Plays Helped Push American Toward Revolution
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Kathleen DuVal

item.title: The Radical Act of Enjoying This Life
item.description: On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the Times Opinion columnist Ezra Klein asks the novelist Gary Shteyngart about his philosophy of enjoyment and why it can feel like such a radical act to simply be present.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: The Teenage Soldier Who Revealed How the Patriots Really Won the War
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Radical Pacifist Who Inspired the First Armed Rebellion of the Founding Era
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Woody Holton

item.title: The Native Leader Who Tied His People’s Fate to the Patriot Cause
item.description: For the 250th, the Times Magazine asked seven leading historians to profile a Revolutionary American whose story has rarely been told.
item.dc:creator: Karim M. Tiro
